verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: Two big unknowns sit dead ahead. Micron reports earnings after the close tonight — the key read on whether the AI/memory-chip selloff keeps going — and Thursday's Core PCE inflation report (8:30 AM) is the week's main event. Markets are trying to steady after Tuesday's global chip rout: futures are mixed-to-slightly-higher and Micron is bouncing a bit pre-market. But volatility is still elevated (VIX ~19.5), the Fed has turned hawkish with hike bets rising, and oil keeps sliding below $72. Take one small, well-supported trade at most, or wait for tonight's print and tomorrow's inflation read.

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · rising into Micron earnings and Thursday's PCE

focus.jtag: Chip rout tries to stabilize; Micron reports AMC; Core PCE lands Thursday

jstats.v: futures mixed · steadying after −2.2% Nasdaq rout

focus.jnote: Today is a "wait for the data" day. Tuesday's global chip selloff (Micron −13%, Qualcomm −8%, Nasdaq −2.2%) is trying to stabilize — Micron and Sandisk bounced about 1% pre-market and South Korea's Kospi rebounded — but the all-clear isn't here. Micron reports after the close tonight, the single biggest read on AI/memory demand, and Core PCE inflation lands Thursday at 8:30 AM. Oil keeps sliding below $72 on the Iran peace roadmap and reopening Hormuz, easing inflation worry, but a hawkish Fed and rising hike odds keep yields firm near 4.5%. Note: Alphabet joins the Dow before Monday's open — a structural change, not a trade. Size down.
focus.notdown: Defensives and large-cap tech outside chips (Walmart, J&J, Microsoft, Amazon) held up Tuesday as money rotated to safety, but that's a flight-to-quality move with no single named catalyst, and it could reverse fast if the chip rout deepens. Low grade.
focus.notup: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) stays the cleaner bearish read: oil is sliding below $72 toward 3-month lows on the Iran 60-day roadmap, the new oil-sales license, and recovering Hormuz traffic — that caps the whole group. Real, durable news. Semis (SMH, NVDA, MU) are still falling, but shorting a fast-dropping group on pure momentum into Micron's earnings tonight is risky. Energy half size; semis watch only.
focus.why: Why YELLOW and not GREEN? The gap-risk numbers (49 and 30%) are still calm enough for a green light on paper — but three rules apply: don't go full size right before a major inflation print (Core PCE is Thursday 8:30 AM), respect a fresh policy shock (the Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes), and don't short a fast-falling market on pure momentum. Micron reports tonight after the close and adds earnings gap-risk to the whole memory complex. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or wait. A cleaner read returns after PCE.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~19.5, elevated and off its lows after the chip rout
Big events (next 1–3 days) — Micron earnings TONIGHT + Core PCE Thursday + final Q1 GDP
World risk — chip-rout aftershock fading; Iran roadmap calming, but no signed final deal yet
Volatility trend — still firm after Tuesday's spike (estimated)
Commodities — oil down toward $72, calming for inflation, not scary

setups.read: Best idea. Oil is sliding below $72 toward 3-month lows on the Iran 60-day roadmap, the new oil-sales license, and recovering Hormuz traffic — that caps the whole group. Real, durable news, but it's pre-PCE, so trade half size.

Reports AMC TONIGHT — earnings risk is in front of it, not behind it, and the stock just fell ~13% on the chip rout. A spread now would straddle the print. Can't use it until the gap is set; revisit Thursday as a possible Type A.

Fell hard on the global chip rout but trying to stabilize (Micron/Sandisk bounced ~1% pre-market). That's momentum, not a clean U.S. catalyst, and Micron earnings tonight sit inside the window. Don't short into it. Wait.
Walmart, J&J, Microsoft, Amazon held up Tuesday as money fled to safety, but it's broad flight-to-quality with no single news story, and it could reverse fast on a hot PCE. Wait.
Newly public, ultra-volatile (~$165, ~25% off its post-IPO peak, just issued $25B in bonds a week after IPO): no real options history, no stable support, wild swings. Can't use it.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Choppy stabilization, then wait for Micron & PCE
scenarios.sub: Chip rout cools but doesn't reverse; everyone waits for tonight's Micron print and Thursday's inflation read

scenarios.label: Good case — Micron reassures, dip-buyers step in
scenarios.sub: A solid Micron report + cheaper oil easing inflation worry → relief bounce in chips and tech

scenarios.label: Bad case — Chip rout resumes, tech rolls over
scenarios.sub: Weak Micron guidance or hike fears stick → broad risk-off, Nasdaq leads down again

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran stumbles or a shock hits
scenarios.sub: The 60-day roadmap breaks down or a hot data point lands → oil and volatility jump

risks: Micron (MU) earnings · tonight AMC — the key read on AI/memory demand after Tuesday's chip rout; a weak guide could reignite the whole semi selloff inside a spread's window.
Core PCE inflation · Thu 6/25 8:30 AM — the Fed's favorite inflation gauge; with the Fed turning hawkish, a hot print could push yields and 2026 hike odds higher.
Hike fears · short end firm — markets price a possible September Fed hike (~68% odds, up from ~29% last week) with the 10-year near 4.5%; growth and tech can drop fast if yields keep climbing.
Chip rout aftershock — Tuesday's global semi selloff (Micron −13%, Qualcomm −8%, Nasdaq −2.2%) is only trying to stabilize; don't short on momentum alone.
Iran deal not signed yet — it's a 60-day roadmap with disputes over nuclear inspectors and Hormuz transit fees persisting; oil could whipsaw on any setback.
